{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "dc8ffa5b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Orbital Congestion Monitoring with Unsupervised Learning\n",
    "\n",
    "This master notebook supports Coco/Ririko's GitHub and Streamlit dashboard project.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Research framing:** This workflow estimates simplified orbital congestion using catalog-level orbital feature similarity. It does **not** calculate real collision probability, conjunction events, or time-dependent orbital propagation.\n",
    "\n",
    "The notebook is designed to be uploaded to GitHub as the single master notebook for the project."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d306439d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Scientific scope\n",
    "\n",
    "This project uses orbital catalog features such as period, inclination, apogee, and perigee to identify objects occupying similar orbital regimes. The main workflow uses:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. data cleaning and feature engineering,\n",
    "2. feature scaling,\n",
    "3. PCA for visualization,\n",
    "4. K-Means clustering,\n",
    "5. nearest-neighbor analysis, and\n",
    "6. a simplified feature-space congestion score.\n",
    "\n",
    "A high congestion score means an object has nearby neighbors in standardized orbital feature space. It does **not** mean a confirmed physical collision will occur."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "182ad352",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:29.418097Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:29.417767Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.312638Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.310992Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "from __future__ import annotations\n",
    "\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "from io import StringIO\n",
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
    "from sklearn.cluster import KMeans\n",
    "from sklearn.decomposition import PCA\n",
    "from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors\n",
    "from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=FutureWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "RANDOM_STATE = 42\n",
    "np.random.seed(RANDOM_STATE)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.rcParams[\"figure.figsize\"] = (10, 6)\n",
    "plt.rcParams[\"font.size\"] = 11"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b923644f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Project paths\n",
    "\n",
    "This cell is written to work whether the notebook is opened from the repository root or from the `notebooks/` folder."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "94ad3379",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.315677Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.315195Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.321275Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.320218Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Project root: /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3\n",
      "Default demo data path: /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/data/coco_demo_current_columns.csv\n",
      "Optional real catalog path: /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/data/satcat.csv\n",
      "Tables will be saved to: /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables\n",
      "Figures will be saved to: /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd()\n",
    "if PROJECT_ROOT.name == \"notebooks\":\n",
    "    PROJECT_ROOT = PROJECT_ROOT.parent\n",
    "\n",
    "DATA_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / \"data\"\n",
    "OUTPUT_TABLE_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / \"outputs\" / \"tables\"\n",
    "OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / \"outputs\" / \"figures\"\n",
    "\n",
    "for folder in [DATA_DIR, OUTPUT_TABLE_DIR, OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR]:\n",
    "    folder.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "DATA_PATH = DATA_DIR / \"satcat.csv\"\n",
    "DEMO_PATH = DATA_DIR / \"coco_demo_current_columns.csv\"\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Project root:\", PROJECT_ROOT)\n",
    "print(\"Default demo data path:\", DEMO_PATH)\n",
    "print(\"Optional real catalog path:\", DATA_PATH)\n",
    "print(\"Tables will be saved to:\", OUTPUT_TABLE_DIR)\n",
    "print(\"Figures will be saved to:\", OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "86ea4e68",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Expected dataset columns\n",
    "\n",
    "The current CSV file should use the following columns:\n",
    "\n",
    "`OBJECT_NAME, OBJECT_ID, NORAD_CAT_ID, OBJECT_TYPE, OPS_STATUS_CODE, OWNER, LAUNCH_DATE, LAUNCH_SITE, DECAY_DATE, PERIOD, INCLINATION, APOGEE, PERIGEE, RCS, DATA_STATUS_CODE, ORBIT_CENTER, ORBIT_TYPE`\n",
    "\n",
    "To analyze a larger real catalog, save it as `data/satcat.csv`. If that file is not present, the notebook uses the demo CSV already included in the repository."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "e446fb79",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.323887Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.323684Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.327046Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.325958Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "EXPECTED_COLUMNS = [\n",
    "    \"OBJECT_NAME\", \"OBJECT_ID\", \"NORAD_CAT_ID\", \"OBJECT_TYPE\", \"OPS_STATUS_CODE\",\n",
    "    \"OWNER\", \"LAUNCH_DATE\", \"LAUNCH_SITE\", \"DECAY_DATE\", \"PERIOD\", \"INCLINATION\",\n",
    "    \"APOGEE\", \"PERIGEE\", \"RCS\", \"DATA_STATUS_CODE\", \"ORBIT_CENTER\", \"ORBIT_TYPE\",\n",
    "]\n",
    "\n",
    "CORE_FEATURES = [\"PERIOD\", \"INCLINATION\", \"MEAN_ALTITUDE\", \"ORBIT_SPREAD\"]\n",
    "REQUESTED_NEIGHBORS = 4"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "74f10a7b",

   "source": [
    "if DATA_PATH.exists():\n",
    "    df_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH)\n",
    "    data_source = DATA_PATH\n",
    "elif DEMO_PATH.exists():\n",
    "    df_raw = pd.read_csv(DEMO_PATH)\n",
    "    data_source = DEMO_PATH\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    raise FileNotFoundError(\n",
    "        \"No dataset found. Add either data/satcat.csv or data/coco_demo_current_columns.csv.\"\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "# Clean column names in case the CSV has accidental spaces around headers.\n",
    "df_raw.columns = [str(col).strip() for col in df_raw.columns]\n",
    "\n",
    "missing_columns = [col for col in EXPECTED_COLUMNS if col not in df_raw.columns]\n",
    "extra_columns = [col for col in df_raw.columns if col not in EXPECTED_COLUMNS]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Data source:\", data_source)\n",
    "print(\"Raw dataset shape:\", df_raw.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Missing expected columns:\", missing_columns if missing_columns else \"None\")\n",
    "print(\"Extra columns:\", extra_columns if extra_columns else \"None\")\n",
    "\n",
    "if missing_columns:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(\"The dataset is missing required columns. Please check the CSV header.\")\n",
    "\n",
    "df_raw.head()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "962acfda",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 5. Clean the dataset and engineer orbital features\n",
    "\n",
    "The raw catalog includes both numerical and categorical fields. For this project, the most important numerical features are orbital period, inclination, apogee, and perigee.\n",
    "\n",
    "Engineered features:\n",
    "\n",
    "- `MEAN_ALTITUDE`: average of apogee and perigee,\n",
    "- `ORBIT_SPREAD`: apogee minus perigee,\n",
    "- `ALTITUDE_RATIO`: apogee divided by perigee,\n",
    "- `IS_DEBRIS`: whether the object is debris,\n",
    "- `IS_PAYLOAD`: whether the object is a payload."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "c55523cb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.355241Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.354981Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.380320Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.379104Z"
    }
   },

      "text/plain": [
       "      OBJECT_NAME OBJECT_TYPE OWNER  ...  PERIGEE  MEAN_ALTITUDE  ORBIT_SPREAD\n",
       "0     ISS (ZARYA)         PAY   ISS  ...    416.0          420.5           9.0\n",
       "1    CSS (TIANHE)         PAY   PRC  ...    376.0          380.5           9.0\n",
       "2      FREGAT DEB         DEB   CIS  ...    757.0         1502.0        1490.0\n",
       "3   CSS (WENTIAN)         PAY   PRC  ...    376.0          380.5           9.0\n",
       "4  CSS (MENGTIAN)         PAY   PRC  ...    376.0          380.5           9.0\n",

     "execution_count": 5,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def normalize_object_type(value):\n",
    "    text = str(value).strip().upper()\n",
    "    if text in {\"PAY\", \"PAYLOAD\", \"SAT\", \"SATELLITE\"}:\n",
    "        return \"PAY\"\n",
    "    if text in {\"DEB\", \"DEBRIS\"}:\n",
    "        return \"DEB\"\n",
    "    if text in {\"R/B\", \"RB\", \"ROCKET BODY\", \"ROCKET_BODY\"}:\n",
    "        return \"R/B\"\n",
    "    if text in {\"UNK\", \"UNKNOWN\", \"NAN\", \"\"}:\n",
    "        return \"UNKNOWN\"\n",
    "    return text\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "df = df_raw.copy()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Ensure every expected column exists, even if a user-loaded file is partially incomplete.\n",
    "for col in EXPECTED_COLUMNS:\n",
    "    if col not in df.columns:\n",
    "        df[col] = np.nan\n",
    "\n",
    "numeric_columns = [\"NORAD_CAT_ID\", \"PERIOD\", \"INCLINATION\", \"APOGEE\", \"PERIGEE\", \"RCS\"]\n",
    "for col in numeric_columns:\n",
    "    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors=\"coerce\")\n",
    "\n",
    "for col in [\"LAUNCH_DATE\", \"DECAY_DATE\"]:\n",
    "    df[col] = pd.to_datetime(df[col], errors=\"coerce\")\n",
    "\n",
    "df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"] = df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].apply(normalize_object_type)\n",
    "df[\"OWNER\"] = df[\"OWNER\"].fillna(\"UNKNOWN\").astype(str).str.strip().replace(\"\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n",
    "df[\"OBJECT_NAME\"] = df[\"OBJECT_NAME\"].fillna(\"Unnamed object\").astype(str).str.strip()\n",
    "\n",
    "df[\"MEAN_ALTITUDE\"] = (df[\"APOGEE\"] + df[\"PERIGEE\"]) / 2\n",
    "df[\"ORBIT_SPREAD\"] = df[\"APOGEE\"] - df[\"PERIGEE\"]\n",
    "df[\"ALTITUDE_RATIO\"] = df[\"APOGEE\"] / df[\"PERIGEE\"].replace(0, np.nan)\n",
    "df[\"HAS_DECAY_DATE\"] = df[\"DECAY_DATE\"].notna().astype(int)\n",
    "df[\"IS_OPERATIONAL\"] = df[\"OPS_STATUS_CODE\"].fillna(\"\").astype(str).str.strip().eq(\"+\").astype(int)\n",
    "df[\"IS_DEBRIS\"] = df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].eq(\"DEB\").astype(int)\n",
    "df[\"IS_PAYLOAD\"] = df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].eq(\"PAY\").astype(int)\n",
    "\n",
    "required_for_analysis = [\"PERIOD\", \"INCLINATION\", \"APOGEE\", \"PERIGEE\", \"MEAN_ALTITUDE\", \"ORBIT_SPREAD\"]\n",
    "analysis_df = df.dropna(subset=required_for_analysis).copy()\n",
    "analysis_df = analysis_df[(analysis_df[\"PERIOD\"] > 0) & (analysis_df[\"APOGEE\"] >= analysis_df[\"PERIGEE\"])]\n",
    "analysis_df = analysis_df.reset_index(drop=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Raw records:\", len(df_raw))\n",
    "print(\"Records with complete orbital features used for analysis:\", len(analysis_df))\n",
    "print(\"Records removed because of missing or invalid orbital features:\", len(df_raw) - len(analysis_df))\n",
    "\n",
    "analysis_df[[\"OBJECT_NAME\", \"OBJECT_TYPE\", \"OWNER\", \"PERIOD\", \"INCLINATION\", \"APOGEE\", \"PERIGEE\", \"MEAN_ALTITUDE\", \"ORBIT_SPREAD\"]].head()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c3cb33a5",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 6. Dataset overview\n",
    "\n",
    "These summaries help connect the dataset to the research question. The object type distribution shows whether the analyzed subset contains payloads, debris, rocket bodies, or other categories. Owner counts are useful for the governance/dashboard discussion, but they should be interpreted carefully because they are catalog labels, not a complete measure of responsibility or legal liability."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "e55be048",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {

   "source": [
    "object_type_counts = analysis_df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].value_counts().reset_index()\n",
    "object_type_counts.columns = [\"OBJECT_TYPE\", \"COUNT\"]\n",
    "owner_counts = analysis_df[\"OWNER\"].value_counts().reset_index()\n",
    "owner_counts.columns = [\"OWNER\", \"COUNT\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "display(object_type_counts)\n",
    "display(owner_counts.head(10))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "07055aa9",

   "source": [
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots()\n",
    "for object_type, group in analysis_df.groupby(\"OBJECT_TYPE\"):\n",
    "    ax.scatter(group[\"INCLINATION\"], group[\"MEAN_ALTITUDE\"], label=object_type, alpha=0.8)\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"Inclination [deg]\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Mean altitude [km]\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Orbital Regimes by Inclination and Mean Altitude\")\n",
    "ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "fig.savefig(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR / \"01_orbital_regimes_altitude_inclination.png\", dpi=200)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a2e6d6d8",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 7. Scale features before clustering\n",
    "\n",
    "The features have different units and ranges, so they must be standardized before K-Means and nearest-neighbor analysis. This prevents one large-scale feature from dominating the distance calculation."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "0e962790",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.681958Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.681765Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.691956Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.690775Z"
    }
   },

     "execution_count": 8,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "if len(analysis_df) < 2:\n",
    "    raise ValueError(\"At least two complete records are required for the unsupervised workflow.\")\n",
    "\n",
    "scaler = StandardScaler()\n",
    "X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(analysis_df[CORE_FEATURES])\n",
    "\n",
    "scaled_preview = pd.DataFrame(X_scaled, columns=[f\"SCALED_{col}\" for col in CORE_FEATURES])\n",
    "scaled_preview.head()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "efd3d644",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 8. Select the number of K-Means clusters\n",
    "\n",
    "The notebook tests several values of `k` and compares inertia and silhouette score. Higher silhouette scores generally indicate clearer cluster separation, but the final choice should also make physical sense."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "2433b24a",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.694422Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:32.694220Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:41.901495Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:41.899658Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Selected k: 2\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/html": [
       "<div>\n",
       "<style scoped>\n",
       "    .dataframe tbody tr th:only-of-type {\n",

       "    }\n",

       "    <tr style=\"text-align: right;\">\n",
       "      <th></th>\n",
       "      <th>k</th>\n",
       "      <th>inertia</th>\n",
       "      <th>silhouette_score</th>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </thead>\n",
       "  <tbody>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>0</th>\n",
       "      <td>2</td>\n",
       "      <td>31.121978</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.845112</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>1</th>\n",
       "      <td>3</td>\n",
       "      <td>3.407039</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.829276</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>2</th>\n",
       "      <td>4</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.895129</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.737854</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>3</th>\n",
       "      <td>5</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.475287</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.697398</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>4</th>\n",
       "      <td>6</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.262136</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.635450</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>5</th>\n",
       "      <td>7</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.180272</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.659046</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>6</th>\n",
       "      <td>8</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.099690</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.701124</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </tbody>\n",
       "</table>\n",
       "</div>"
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "   k    inertia  silhouette_score\n",
       "0  2  31.121978          0.845112\n",
       "1  3   3.407039          0.829276\n",
       "2  4   0.895129          0.737854\n",
       "3  5   0.475287          0.697398\n",
       "4  6   0.262136          0.635450\n",
       "5  7   0.180272          0.659046\n",
       "6  8   0.099690          0.701124"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 9,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "max_k = min(8, len(analysis_df) - 1)\n",
    "cluster_rows = []\n",
    "\n",
    "if max_k >= 2:\n",
    "    for k in range(2, max_k + 1):\n",
    "        model = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_init=10)\n",
    "        labels = model.fit_predict(X_scaled)\n",
    "        score = silhouette_score(X_scaled, labels) if len(set(labels)) > 1 else np.nan\n",
    "        cluster_rows.append({\"k\": k, \"inertia\": model.inertia_, \"silhouette_score\": score})\n",
    "\n",
    "    cluster_eval = pd.DataFrame(cluster_rows)\n",
    "    best_row = cluster_eval.sort_values(\"silhouette_score\", ascending=False).iloc[0]\n",
    "    BEST_K = int(best_row[\"k\"])\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    cluster_eval = pd.DataFrame([{\"k\": 1, \"inertia\": 0.0, \"silhouette_score\": np.nan}])\n",
    "    BEST_K = 1\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Selected k:\", BEST_K)\n",
    "cluster_eval"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "f08b2e8d",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:41.904245Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:41.904007Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:42.359060Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:42.357734Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [

      "image/png": "",

       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},

    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",

       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots()\n",
    "ax.plot(cluster_eval[\"k\"], cluster_eval[\"inertia\"], marker=\"o\")\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"Number of clusters k\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Inertia\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Elbow Curve for K-Means Clustering\")\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "fig.savefig(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR / \"02_kmeans_elbow_curve.png\", dpi=200)\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots()\n",
    "ax.plot(cluster_eval[\"k\"], cluster_eval[\"silhouette_score\"], marker=\"o\")\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"Number of clusters k\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Silhouette score\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Silhouette Score by Cluster Number\")\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "fig.savefig(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR / \"03_silhouette_scores.png\", dpi=200)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1f159a55",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 9. Fit final K-Means model and visualize with PCA\n",
    "\n",
    "K-Means assigns each object to a cluster based on similarity in the standardized orbital feature space. PCA is used only for visualization. If PCA captures a high percentage of variance, it means the 2D figure preserves much of the structure of the selected features. It does **not** prove the model predicts real collision probability."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "1cd9b65f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:42.361670Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:42.361453Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.293652Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.292378Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "PCA variance captured by PC1 + PC2: 98.93%\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/html": [
       "<div>\n",
       "<style scoped>\n",
       "    .dataframe tbody tr th:only-of-type {\n",
       "        vertical-align: middle;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "\n",
       "    .dataframe tbody tr th {\n",
       "        vertical-align: top;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "\n",
       "    .dataframe thead th {\n",
       "        text-align: right;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "</style>\n",
       "<table border=\"1\" class=\"dataframe\">\n",
       "  <thead>\n",
       "    <tr style=\"text-align: right;\">\n",
       "      <th></th>\n",
       "      <th>OBJECT_NAME</th>\n",
       "      <th>OBJECT_TYPE</th>\n",
       "      <th>OWNER</th>\n",
       "      <th>CLUSTER</th>\n",
       "      <th>PC1</th>\n",
       "      <th>PC2</th>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </thead>\n",
       "  <tbody>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>0</th>\n",
       "      <td>ISS (ZARYA)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>ISS</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>-0.048803</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.527352</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>1</th>\n",
       "      <td>CSS (TIANHE)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>-0.470502</td>\n",
       "      <td>-1.881181</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>2</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>1</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.826012</td>\n",
       "      <td>-0.244807</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>3</th>\n",
       "      <td>CSS (WENTIAN)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>-0.470502</td>\n",
       "      <td>-1.881181</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>4</th>\n",
       "      <td>CSS (MENGTIAN)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>-0.470502</td>\n",
       "      <td>-1.881181</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </tbody>\n",
       "</table>\n",
       "</div>"
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "      OBJECT_NAME OBJECT_TYPE OWNER CLUSTER       PC1       PC2\n",
       "0     ISS (ZARYA)         PAY   ISS       0 -0.048803  0.527352\n",
       "1    CSS (TIANHE)         PAY   PRC       0 -0.470502 -1.881181\n",
       "2      FREGAT DEB         DEB   CIS       1  8.826012 -0.244807\n",
       "3   CSS (WENTIAN)         PAY   PRC       0 -0.470502 -1.881181\n",
       "4  CSS (MENGTIAN)         PAY   PRC       0 -0.470502 -1.881181"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 11,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "if BEST_K >= 2:\n",
    "    final_model = KMeans(n_clusters=BEST_K, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_init=10)\n",
    "    analysis_df[\"CLUSTER\"] = final_model.fit_predict(X_scaled).astype(str)\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    analysis_df[\"CLUSTER\"] = \"0\"\n",
    "\n",
    "pca = PCA(n_components=2, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)\n",
    "X_pca = pca.fit_transform(X_scaled)\n",
    "analysis_df[\"PC1\"] = X_pca[:, 0]\n",
    "analysis_df[\"PC2\"] = X_pca[:, 1]\n",
    "analysis_df[\"PCA_VARIANCE_SUM\"] = pca.explained_variance_ratio_.sum()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"PCA variance captured by PC1 + PC2: {analysis_df['PCA_VARIANCE_SUM'].iloc[0] * 100:.2f}%\")\n",
    "analysis_df[[\"OBJECT_NAME\", \"OBJECT_TYPE\", \"OWNER\", \"CLUSTER\", \"PC1\", \"PC2\"]].head()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "1bd18f07",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.296622Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.296386Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.597289Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.595951Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots()\n",
    "for cluster, group in analysis_df.groupby(\"CLUSTER\"):\n",
    "    ax.scatter(group[\"PC1\"], group[\"PC2\"], label=f\"Cluster {cluster}\", alpha=0.8)\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"PC1\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"PC2\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"PCA View of Orbital Feature Clusters\")\n",
    "ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "fig.savefig(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR / \"04_pca_cluster_map.png\", dpi=200)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "442e3822",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 10. Nearest-neighbor analysis and the `(28, 4)` issue\n",
    "\n",
    "Nearest-neighbor analysis finds the closest objects in standardized orbital feature space.\n",
    "\n",
    "If the output shape is `(28, 4)`, this means:\n",
    "\n",
    "- 28 complete orbital records were used after cleaning, and\n",
    "- 4 nearest neighbors were requested for each object.\n",
    "\n",
    "This is expected. It is not an error and does not mean there are only four data points."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 13,
   "id": "f645524e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.600481Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.600289Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.606649Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.605699Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Nearest-neighbor distance matrix shape: (28, 4)\n",
      "Interpretation: 28 analyzed objects × 4 nearest neighbors per object.\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "neighbor_count = min(REQUESTED_NEIGHBORS, len(analysis_df) - 1)\n",
    "nearest_model = NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=neighbor_count + 1, metric=\"euclidean\")\n",
    "nearest_model.fit(X_scaled)\n",
    "\n",
    "distances_with_self, indices_with_self = nearest_model.kneighbors(X_scaled)\n",
    "\n",
    "# The first neighbor is each object itself, so remove that first column.\n",
    "neighbor_distances = distances_with_self[:, 1:]\n",
    "neighbor_indices = indices_with_self[:, 1:]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Nearest-neighbor distance matrix shape:\", neighbor_distances.shape)\n",
    "print(\n",
    "    \"Interpretation:\",\n",
    "    f\"{neighbor_distances.shape[0]} analyzed objects × {neighbor_distances.shape[1]} nearest neighbors per object.\"\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d1ad500e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 11. Simplified congestion score\n",
    "\n",
    "The simplified congestion score is based on the average distance to nearby neighbors in standardized feature space.\n",
    "\n",
    "Objects with smaller average neighbor distances receive higher congestion scores. This indicates local crowding in feature space, not confirmed collision risk."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "8ea72a88",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.609528Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.609265Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.625111Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.623697Z"
    }
   },

       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",

      "text/plain": [
       "       OBJECT_NAME OBJECT_TYPE  ... CONGESTION_SCORE CONGESTION_CATEGORY\n",
       "0      ISS (ZARYA)         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "1     CSS (TIANHE)         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "3    CSS (WENTIAN)         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "4   CSS (MENGTIAN)         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "6   PROGRESS-MS 32         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "5       TIANZHOU-9         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "11     SHENZHOU-22         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "12     SOYUZ-MS 28         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",
       "26  PROGRESS-MS 33         PAY  ...        1000000.0                High\n",

       "[10 rows x 7 columns]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 14,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "analysis_df[\"AVG_NN_DISTANCE\"] = neighbor_distances.mean(axis=1)\n",
    "analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"] = 1 / (analysis_df[\"AVG_NN_DISTANCE\"] + 1e-6)\n",
    "analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_PERCENTILE\"] = analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"].rank(pct=True) * 100\n",
    "\n",
    "high_cutoff = analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"].quantile(0.75)\n",
    "medium_cutoff = analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"].quantile(0.40)\n",
    "analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_CATEGORY\"] = np.select(\n",
    "    [\n",
    "        analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"] >= high_cutoff,\n",
    "        analysis_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"] >= medium_cutoff,\n",
    "    ],\n",
    "    [\"High\", \"Medium\"],\n",
    "    default=\"Low\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "top_congested = analysis_df.sort_values(\"CONGESTION_SCORE\", ascending=False).head(10)\n",
    "top_congested[[\"OBJECT_NAME\", \"OBJECT_TYPE\", \"OWNER\", \"CLUSTER\", \"AVG_NN_DISTANCE\", \"CONGESTION_SCORE\", \"CONGESTION_CATEGORY\"]]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "abe668d0",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.627269Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.627046Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.852724Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.851301Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "plot_df = top_congested.sort_values(\"CONGESTION_SCORE\")\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots()\n",
    "ax.barh(plot_df[\"OBJECT_NAME\"], plot_df[\"CONGESTION_SCORE\"])\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"Simplified congestion score\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Object\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Top Objects by Feature-Space Congestion Score\")\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "fig.savefig(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR / \"05_top_congestion_scores.png\", dpi=200)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "84adfdfb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 12. Nearest-neighbor table\n",
    "\n",
    "This table is useful for the Streamlit dashboard and for interpreting which objects are near each other in feature space."

       "      <td>4</td>\n",
       "      <td>TIANZHOU-9</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.000000</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>8</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>1</td>\n",
       "      <td>HTV-X1</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>JPN</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.671400</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>9</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>2</td>\n",
       "      <td>SOYUZ-MS 28</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.915673</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>10</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>3</td>\n",
       "      <td>ISS (ZARYA)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>ISS</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.915673</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>11</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>4</td>\n",
       "      <td>PROGRESS-MS 32</td>\n",
       "      <td>PAY</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.915673</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </tbody>\n",
       "</table>\n",
       "</div>"
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "     OBJECT_NAME OBJECT_TYPE  ... NEIGHBOR_OWNER  FEATURE_SPACE_DISTANCE\n",
       "0    ISS (ZARYA)         PAY  ...            CIS                0.000000\n",
       "1    ISS (ZARYA)         PAY  ...            CIS                0.000000\n",
       "2    ISS (ZARYA)         PAY  ...            ISS                0.000000\n",
       "3    ISS (ZARYA)         PAY  ...             US                0.000000\n",
       "4   CSS (TIANHE)         PAY  ...            PRC                0.000000\n",
       "5   CSS (TIANHE)         PAY  ...            PRC                0.000000\n",
       "6   CSS (TIANHE)         PAY  ...            PRC                0.000000\n",
       "7   CSS (TIANHE)         PAY  ...            PRC                0.000000\n",
       "8     FREGAT DEB         DEB  ...            JPN                8.671400\n",
       "9     FREGAT DEB         DEB  ...            CIS                8.915673\n",
       "10    FREGAT DEB         DEB  ...            ISS                8.915673\n",
       "11    FREGAT DEB         DEB  ...            CIS                8.915673\n",
       "\n",
       "[12 rows x 8 columns]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 16,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "neighbor_records = []\n",
    "for row_i, object_row in analysis_df.iterrows():\n",
    "    for rank, (neighbor_i, distance) in enumerate(zip(neighbor_indices[row_i], neighbor_distances[row_i]), start=1):\n",
    "        neighbor_row = analysis_df.iloc[int(neighbor_i)]\n",
    "        neighbor_records.append({\n",
    "            \"OBJECT_NAME\": object_row[\"OBJECT_NAME\"],\n",
    "            \"OBJECT_TYPE\": object_row[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"],\n",
    "            \"OWNER\": object_row[\"OWNER\"],\n",
    "            \"NEIGHBOR_RANK\": rank,\n",
    "            \"NEIGHBOR_NAME\": neighbor_row[\"OBJECT_NAME\"],\n",
    "            \"NEIGHBOR_TYPE\": neighbor_row[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"],\n",
    "            \"NEIGHBOR_OWNER\": neighbor_row[\"OWNER\"],\n",
    "            \"FEATURE_SPACE_DISTANCE\": float(distance),\n",
    "        })\n",
    "\n",
    "nearest_neighbor_table = pd.DataFrame(neighbor_records)\n",
    "nearest_neighbor_table.head(12)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "20c271ba",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 13. Debris-payload proximity pairs\n",
    "\n",
    "This step identifies debris objects and their nearest payload objects in standardized orbital feature space. These pairs can be used as monitoring examples in the dashboard, but they are not confirmed physical close approaches."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 17,
   "id": "dc908788",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.877938Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.877741Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.892000Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.890901Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/html": [
       "<div>\n",
       "<style scoped>\n",
       "    .dataframe tbody tr th:only-of-type {\n",
       "        vertical-align: middle;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "\n",
       "    .dataframe tbody tr th {\n",
       "        vertical-align: top;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "\n",
       "    .dataframe thead th {\n",
       "        text-align: right;\n",
       "    }\n",
       "</style>\n",
       "<table border=\"1\" class=\"dataframe\">\n",
       "  <thead>\n",
       "    <tr style=\"text-align: right;\">\n",
       "      <th></th>\n",
       "      <th>DEBRIS_OBJECT</th>\n",
       "      <th>DEBRIS_OWNER</th>\n",
       "      <th>NEAREST_PAYLOAD</th>\n",
       "      <th>PAYLOAD_OWNER</th>\n",
       "      <th>FEATURE_SPACE_DISTANCE</th>\n",
       "      <th>DEBRIS_CLUSTER</th>\n",
       "      <th>PAYLOAD_CLUSTER</th>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </thead>\n",
       "  <tbody>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>1</th>\n",
       "      <td>HRC MONOBLOCK CAMERA</td>\n",
       "      <td>CA</td>\n",
       "      <td>ISS OBJECT XY</td>\n",
       "      <td>TBD</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.109111</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>2</th>\n",
       "      <td>SZ-21 MODULE</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>CSS (TIANHE)</td>\n",
       "      <td>PRC</td>\n",
       "      <td>0.168873</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "    <tr>\n",
       "      <th>0</th>\n",
       "      <td>FREGAT DEB</td>\n",
       "      <td>CIS</td>\n",
       "      <td>HTV-X1</td>\n",
       "      <td>JPN</td>\n",
       "      <td>8.671400</td>\n",
       "      <td>1</td>\n",
       "      <td>0</td>\n",
       "    </tr>\n",
       "  </tbody>\n",
       "</table>\n",
       "</div>"
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "          DEBRIS_OBJECT DEBRIS_OWNER  ... DEBRIS_CLUSTER PAYLOAD_CLUSTER\n",
       "1  HRC MONOBLOCK CAMERA           CA  ...              0               0\n",
       "2          SZ-21 MODULE          PRC  ...              0               0\n",
       "0            FREGAT DEB          CIS  ...              1               0\n",
       "\n",
       "[3 rows x 7 columns]"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 17,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "payload_positions = analysis_df.index[analysis_df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].eq(\"PAY\")].to_numpy()\n",
    "debris_positions = analysis_df.index[analysis_df[\"OBJECT_TYPE\"].eq(\"DEB\")].to_numpy()\n",
    "\n",
    "pair_records = []\n",
    "if len(payload_positions) > 0 and len(debris_positions) > 0:\n",
    "    payload_scaled = X_scaled[payload_positions]\n",
    "    debris_scaled = X_scaled[debris_positions]\n",
    "    payload_nn = NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=1, metric=\"euclidean\")\n",
    "    payload_nn.fit(payload_scaled)\n",
    "    pair_distances, pair_indices = payload_nn.kneighbors(debris_scaled)\n",
    "\n",
    "    for debris_pos, local_payload_index, distance in zip(debris_positions, pair_indices[:, 0], pair_distances[:, 0]):\n",
    "        payload_pos = payload_positions[int(local_payload_index)]\n",
    "        debris_row = analysis_df.iloc[int(debris_pos)]\n",
    "        payload_row = analysis_df.iloc[int(payload_pos)]\n",
    "        pair_records.append({\n",
    "            \"DEBRIS_OBJECT\": debris_row[\"OBJECT_NAME\"],\n",
    "            \"DEBRIS_OWNER\": debris_row[\"OWNER\"],\n",
    "            \"NEAREST_PAYLOAD\": payload_row[\"OBJECT_NAME\"],\n",
    "            \"PAYLOAD_OWNER\": payload_row[\"OWNER\"],\n",
    "            \"FEATURE_SPACE_DISTANCE\": float(distance),\n",
    "            \"DEBRIS_CLUSTER\": debris_row[\"CLUSTER\"],\n",
    "            \"PAYLOAD_CLUSTER\": payload_row[\"CLUSTER\"],\n",
    "        })\n",
    "\n",
    "debris_payload_pairs = pd.DataFrame(pair_records)\n",
    "if not debris_payload_pairs.empty:\n",
    "    debris_payload_pairs = debris_payload_pairs.sort_values(\"FEATURE_SPACE_DISTANCE\")\n",
    "\n",
    "debris_payload_pairs"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "68a37669",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 14. Cluster summary\n",
    "\n",
    "This summary helps connect the numerical clusters back to physical orbital regimes."
   ]
  },

      "text/plain": [

       "0       0            27  ...             2             25\n",

      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 18,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "cluster_summary = (\n",
    "    analysis_df.groupby(\"CLUSTER\")\n",
    "    .agg(\n",
    "        OBJECT_COUNT=(\"OBJECT_NAME\", \"count\"),\n",
    "        MEAN_PERIOD=(\"PERIOD\", \"mean\"),\n",
    "        MEAN_INCLINATION=(\"INCLINATION\", \"mean\"),\n",
    "        MEAN_ALTITUDE=(\"MEAN_ALTITUDE\", \"mean\"),\n",
    "        MEAN_ORBIT_SPREAD=(\"ORBIT_SPREAD\", \"mean\"),\n",
    "        MEAN_CONGESTION_SCORE=(\"CONGESTION_SCORE\", \"mean\"),\n",
    "        DEBRIS_COUNT=(\"IS_DEBRIS\", \"sum\"),\n",
    "        PAYLOAD_COUNT=(\"IS_PAYLOAD\", \"sum\"),\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    .reset_index()\n",
    "    .sort_values(\"CLUSTER\")\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "cluster_summary"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "29f6c90a",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 15. Save dashboard-ready files\n",
    "\n",
    "The Streamlit app can recreate the analysis from the raw CSV, but saving the tables is useful for reproducibility and for paper writing."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 19,
   "id": "941a7fc7",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.914553Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.914382Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.925102Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-02T06:12:43.923947Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Saved tables:\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/dashboard_ready_orbital_congestion.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/nearest_neighbor_table.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/debris_payload_pairs.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/cluster_evaluation.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/cluster_summary.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/object_type_counts.csv\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/tables/owner_counts.csv\n",
      "\n",
      "Saved figures:\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures/01_orbital_regimes_altitude_inclination.png\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures/02_kmeans_elbow_curve.png\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures/03_silhouette_scores.png\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures/04_pca_cluster_map.png\n",
      "- /mnt/data/coco_orbital_congestion_dashboard_v3/outputs/figures/05_top_congestion_scores.png\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Convert dates to strings before CSV export.\n",
    "export_df = analysis_df.copy()\n",
    "for date_col in [\"LAUNCH_DATE\", \"DECAY_DATE\"]:\n",
    "    export_df[date_col] = pd.to_datetime(export_df[date_col], errors=\"coerce\").dt.strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d\")\n",
    "\n",
    "output_tables = {\n",
    "    \"dashboard_ready_orbital_congestion.csv\": export_df,\n",
    "    \"nearest_neighbor_table.csv\": nearest_neighbor_table,\n",
    "    \"debris_payload_pairs.csv\": debris_payload_pairs,\n",
    "    \"cluster_evaluation.csv\": cluster_eval,\n",
    "    \"cluster_summary.csv\": cluster_summary,\n",
    "    \"object_type_counts.csv\": object_type_counts,\n",
    "    \"owner_counts.csv\": owner_counts,\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "for filename, table in output_tables.items():\n",
    "    table.to_csv(OUTPUT_TABLE_DIR / filename, index=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Saved tables:\")\n",
    "for filename in output_tables:\n",
    "    print(\"-\", OUTPUT_TABLE_DIR / filename)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\nSaved figures:\")\n",
    "for figure_path in sorted(OUTPUT_FIGURE_DIR.glob(\"*.png\")):\n",
    "    print(\"-\", figure_path)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "cc2cd520",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 16. Paper-ready interpretation\n",
    "\n",
    "Use careful wording in the paper:\n",
    "\n",
    "- Correct: “This study estimates simplified orbital congestion using feature-space similarity.”\n",
    "- Correct: “Objects with similar orbital features may be useful monitoring candidates.”\n",
    "- Incorrect: “This model predicts real collision probability.”\n",
    "\n",
    "The workflow is valuable because it is interpretable, lightweight, and dashboard-ready, but it should be presented as a screening and visualization tool rather than a full space-traffic-management system."
   ]
  }
 ],
